CUL 110.title: Sanitation and Safety
CUL 110.desc: Introduces the basic principles of sanitation and safety relative to the hospitality industry, including ServSafe-style food handling and workplace safety.

CUL 112.title: Nutrition for Foodservice
CUL 112.desc: Covers nutrition principles and their application to foodservice operations and menu planning.

CUL 135.title: Food and Beverage Service
CUL 135.desc: Teaches practical skills for effective food and beverage service across various hospitality settings.

CUL 135A.title: Food and Beverage Service Lab
CUL 135A.desc: Laboratory experience enhancing skills in guest service, dining-room setup, and beverage service techniques.

CUL 140.title: Culinary Skills I
CUL 140.desc: Introduces fundamental cooking concepts, techniques, and heat methods for basic culinary applications.

CUL 140A.title: Culinary Skills I Lab
CUL 140A.desc: Hands-on practice in basic cooking skills, knife techniques, and sauce preparation.

CUL 150.title: Food Science
CUL 150.desc: Explores the chemical and physical changes in food during cooking, handling, and processing.

CUL 160.title: Baking I
CUL 160.desc: Covers basic baking ingredients and techniques, including yeast and chemically leavened products.

CUL 160A.title: Baking I Lab
CUL 160A.desc: Laboratory experience developing proficiency in bakeshop applications and pastry preparation.

CUL 170.title: Garde Manger I
CUL 170.desc: Introduces cold food preparation including salads, sandwiches, appetizers, and pantry production.

CUL 170A.title: Garde Manger I Lab
CUL 170A.desc: Hands-on practice in cold-kitchen skills and cold food display design.

CUL 230.title: Global Cuisines
CUL 230.desc: Develops practical experience planning, preparing, and presenting representative foods from world cuisines.

CUL 230A.title: Global Cuisines Lab
CUL 230A.desc: Laboratory work with global cuisines emphasizing historical, geographical, and technique influences.

CUL 240.title: Culinary Skills II
CUL 240.desc: Advances cooking fundamentals including meat fabrication, sauces, and plate presentation.

CUL 240A.title: Culinary Skills II Lab
CUL 240A.desc: Laboratory practice in meat preparation, butchery, and entree-with-sides production.

CUL 260.title: Baking II
CUL 260.desc: Furthers baking knowledge including classical desserts, cake decorating, and presentation.

CUL 260A.title: Baking II Lab
CUL 260A.desc: Hands-on practice in cake decorating, pastry production, and dessert plating.

CUL 270.title: Garde Manger II
CUL 270.desc: Advances cold-food techniques including pates, terrines, charcuterie, and classical cold buffet design.

CUL 270A.title: Garde Manger II Lab
CUL 270A.desc: Laboratory work developing advanced garde manger skills and cold buffet showpieces.

CUL 273.title: Career Development
CUL 273.desc: Introduces career planning, self-assessment, portfolio development, and professional job-search strategies.

CUL 283.title: Farm-to-Table
CUL 283.desc: Explores sustainable farming partnerships, local food sourcing, and environmental stewardship.

BPA 120.title: Petit Fours and Pastries
BPA 120.desc: Introduces the preparation and plating of petit fours and individual dessert pastries for buffets and events.

BPA 120A.title: Petit Fours and Pastries Lab
BPA 120A.desc: Lab experience producing petit fours and pastries using modern production methods.

BPA 140.title: Modern Cakes and Entremets
BPA 140.desc: Teaches modern cake production and decor including mixing methods, fillings, icing, and entremet assembly.

BPA 142.title: Culinary Fundamentals
BPA 142.desc: Overview of culinary techniques for bakery and cafe settings, emphasizing mise en place and knife skills.

BPA 150.title: Artisan and Specialty Bread
BPA 150.desc: Advanced bread making covering naturally leavened loaves, hearth breads, focaccia, and specialty grains.

BPA 170.title: Cold Desserts and Components
BPA 170.desc: Fundamentals of cold and frozen desserts including ice creams, sorbets, sauces, and garnishes.

BPA 210.title: Cake Design and Decorating
BPA 210.desc: Advanced cake design and decoration including tiered cakes, fondant, gum paste, and piping techniques.

BPA 210A.title: Cake Design and Decorating Lab
BPA 210A.desc: Lab experience building multi-tier specialty cakes with fondant and gum paste.

BPA 245.title: Plated Dessert Development
BPA 245.desc: Planning and execution of multi-component plated desserts emphasizing texture, balance, and presentation.

BPA 250.title: Dessert and Bread Production
BPA 250.desc: Merges artistry with commercial bread and dessert production, including costing and pricing.

BPA 255.title: Chocolates and Confections
BPA 255.desc: Chocolate and sugar confections including tempering, molding, filling, enrobing, and candy types.

BPA 260.title: Pastry and Baking Marketing
BPA 260.desc: Marketing concepts for bakery and pastry operations including menu planning, pricing, and advertising.

BPA 260A.title: Pastry and Baking Marketing Lab
BPA 260A.desc: Lab applying costing, yield, purchasing, and social-media/print marketing for bakery businesses.

HRM 110.title: Introduction to Hospitality and Tourism
HRM 110.desc: Explores hospitality industry growth, tourism, lodging, gaming, restaurants, and career pathways.

HRM 120.title: Front Office Procedures
HRM 120.desc: Covers lodging front-office operations including reservations, registration, and guest-services systems.

HRM 125.title: Etiquette for Hospitality
HRM 125.desc: Teaches social skills for professional interactions, etiquette, and business situations.

HRM 140.title: Legal Issues-Hospitality
HRM 140.desc: Addresses legal responsibilities, regulations, safety, risk management, and liability prevention.

HRM 150.title: Training for Hospitality
HRM 150.desc: Introduces training program development, job specifications, coaching, and management techniques.

HRM 210.title: Meetings and Event Planning
HRM 210.desc: Covers planning and operating conventions, trade shows, meetings, and foodservice events.

HRM 220.title: Cost Control-Food and Beverage
HRM 220.desc: Examines cost-control accounting, financial statements, labor scheduling, and operational efficiency.

HRM 225.title: Beverage Management
HRM 225.desc: Introduces beverage management including wines, spirits, service, and responsible alcohol practices.

HRM 230.title: Club and Resort Management
HRM 230.desc: Presents resort and club management principles, marketing, and recreational-activity oversight.

HRM 240.title: Marketing for Hospitality
HRM 240.desc: Teaches marketing planning, target strategies, advertising, public relations, and sales techniques.

HRM 245.title: Human Resource Management-Hospitality
HRM 245.desc: Covers human resource management including recruitment, training, evaluation, and labor laws.

HRM 280.title: Management Problems-Hospitality
HRM 280.desc: Addresses current industry challenges through problem-solving and managerial decision-making.

COS 111.title: Cosmetology Concepts I
COS 111.desc: Introduces basic cosmetology concepts including safety, sanitation, bacteriology, anatomy, and ethics.

COS 112.title: Salon I
COS 112.desc: Introduces fundamental salon services such as shampooing, hair color, haircutting, and permanent waving.

COS 113.title: Cosmetology Concepts II
COS 113.desc: Covers product knowledge, chemistry, manicuring, and chemical hair restructuring.

COS 114.title: Salon II
COS 114.desc: Simulated salon experience in skin care, manicuring, hair color, design, and cutting.

COS 115.title: Cosmetology Concepts III
COS 115.desc: Addresses salon management, salesmanship, skin care, and thermal hair styling.

COS 116.title: Salon III
COS 116.desc: Intermediate simulated salon practice in skin care, manicuring, coloring, and design.

COS 117.title: Cosmetology Concepts IV
COS 117.desc: Advanced concepts on chemistry, hair structure, and licensing-exam preparation.

COS 118.title: Salon IV
COS 118.desc: Advanced simulated salon experience preparing students for the licensing exam and employment.

COS 223.title: Contemporary Hair Coloring
COS 223.desc: Teaches color theory and contemporary techniques and application methods for hair coloring.

COS 240.title: Contemporary Design
COS 240.desc: Covers contemporary design methods and techniques for modern styling approaches.

HOR 112.title: Landscape Design I
HOR 112.desc: Landscape principles and practices for residential and commercial sites, including drafting and plant selection.

HOR 116.title: Landscape Management I
HOR 116.desc: Analyze landscape properties and develop management schedules based on plant condition and client needs.

HOR 124.title: Nursery Operations
HOR 124.desc: Nursery site selection, crop production, irrigation, fertilization, and marketing for growers.

HOR 134.title: Greenhouse Operations
HOR 134.desc: Greenhouse operations including environmental control, record keeping, scheduling, and crop production.

HOR 150.title: Introduction to Horticulture
HOR 150.desc: Fundamental horticulture techniques including propagation, planting, watering, pest control, and design.

HOR 160.title: Plant Materials I
HOR 160.desc: Identification and culture of landscape plants, emphasizing nomenclature and growth requirements.

HOR 161.title: Plant Materials II
HOR 161.desc: Extended study of landscape plants, nomenclature, and sustainable landscape applications.

HOR 162.title: Applied Plant Science
HOR 162.desc: Applies botany to horticulture including physiology, morphology, and anatomy for plant culture.

HOR 164.title: Horticultural Pest Management
HOR 164.desc: Pest identification and least-toxic management; prepares for the NC Commercial Pesticide license exam.

HOR 166.title: Soils and Fertilizers
HOR 166.desc: Soil properties, fertility management, testing, and fertilizer application using sustainable practices.

HOR 168.title: Plant Propagation
HOR 168.desc: Sexual and asexual plant reproduction including seed propagation, grafting, and micro-propagation.

HOR 257.title: Arboriculture Practices
HOR 257.desc: Tree and shrub culture including fertilization, pruning, climbing, pest control, and equipment safety.

TRF 110.title: Introduction Turfgrass Cultivation and Identification
TRF 110.desc: Introduces turfgrass species identification and the fundamentals of turf cultivation and care.
